target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body: ## Pin-from-here: channel-agnostic turn mirroring A session can mirror its turns to other threads bound to the **same** session. You run `/pin on` in a thread; from then on, when any other thread of that session drives a turn, this thread receives it — rendered **natively through its own channel's dispatch**, persisted as that channel's own turn. ### Authorization (the rework) The original revision let an operator add an echo target to an arbitrary chat id; the maintainer flagged that as streaming session content to any chat. This revision removes that surface entirely: - **`/pin`** is in-chat, opt-in, and binds only the session already bound to *this* thread — every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that session. There is no "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path. - Operator echo adds require a session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), fail-closed. ### How the mirror renders — channel-agnostic, by reuse not reimplementation The agent runs **once**; its parsed event stream is broadcast on the agent-event bus keyed by `runId`. For each pinned target, the core hands that target's **own channel dispatch** a bus-sourced `replyResolver` (`createMirrorReplyResolver`) that replays the origin run's stream instead of calling the model: - `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` already accepts a pluggable `replyResolver`. `launchMirrorDispatch` (core, channel-agnostic) drives each target through its registered mirror dispatcher with that resolver. - Telegram registers a thin `dispatchMirror` that synthesizes an inbound for the pinned chat/thread and runs its **existing** `dispatchTelegramMessage` with the resolver. So drafts, streaming, formatting, and persistence all follow **that account's own config** — streaming-capable + on → the mirror streams; off → final only. Nothing is forced; nothing is reimplemented per channel. - **Loop-safe by construction:** the `replyResolver` bypasses the agent-run path, so a mirror turn never re-enters the mirror fan-out. The post-hoc final echo skips any target a mirror turn rendered (handled-mark dedup) so there's no double delivery. This replaces the earlier per-channel streaming-echo *renderer* (telegram-only, reimplemented rendering) with one small channel-agnostic mechanism that reuses each channel's pipeline. **Net −894 LOC**: deletes `echo-renderer*`, `echo-streaming`, `streaming-progress-callbacks`, the `registerEchoRendererFactory` plugin-api plumbing, and the `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath; adds a small core launcher/registry (reusing the existing `echo-mirror-resolver`) plus a thin telegram adapter. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) - **Behavior or issue addressed**: Pin-from-here mirrors a session turn into pinned target threads by re-running each target channel's own dispatch with a bus-sourced reply resolver, so the mirror renders + persists natively (streaming/verbose per that channel's own config) instead of a flat post-hoc echo. - **Real environment tested**: A real OpenClaw gateway built from this branch in a Linux crabbox with a desktop; a real Telegram bot + a real Telegram account on Telegram Desktop; a real `claude-cli` backend (`sonnet-4-6`/`opus-4-8`, live Anthropic API) with exec tools; the tdlib user-driver sending real messages. This recreates the Mantis telegram-desktop proof flow locally (same mechanism: telegram-desktop + user-driver + screen capture). - **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started the gateway from the built branch; in a Telegram thread sent `/new`, `/verbose on`, `/pin on`; then drove the same session from webchat (`chat.send`) with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname`, and a two-step `date -u` + `uname -a` narrated before each command); screen-recorded Telegram Desktop and captured the gateway debug log. - **Evidence after fix**: Screenshots on fork branch `proof/pin-from-here-evidence` (linked below) plus gateway-log excerpts — `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn and the telegram mirror runs the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`), exactly once (post-hoc echo skipped by the handled-mark). - **Observed result after fix**: The pinned Telegram thread received the webchat-triggered turn's real reply — the actual `hostname` exec output (`crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`) — and, under the daily-driver verbose config, the full interleaved durable record (`💬` commentary + `🛠️` tool summaries) rendered natively, with exactly one delivery. - **What was not tested**: A *live* current-head capture of **destination revocation** (the gate is unit-proven and the post-hoc fall-through is closed, but the test-rig group-disable mechanism proved finicky to film). Multi-account routing IS now proven on current head — see below. #### Multi-account routing — current head (account-aware dispatch) Two real Telegram accounts in one gateway: `default` = bot #2 in GID `-1003900553563`, `acct2` = bot #3 (`@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot`) in a separate forum group GID2 `-1004389798714` (bot #3 promoted admin; bot #2 is **not** a member of GID2). The echo target for GID2 is set with `accountId: acct2`, then a **webchat-origin** turn drives the session. Gateway debug log (`.proof/pin-from-here-multiaccount-log.txt`): ```text [telegram] [acct2] starting provider (@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot) [telegram] [default] starting provider (@JeeevesOpenClawTelegram2bot) message received: channel=webchat ... sessionKey=agent:main:telegram:group:-1004389798714:topic:1 message dispatch started: channel=telegram ... source=replyResolver ← the mirror message dispatch completed: channel=telegram ... source=replyResolver outcome=completed ``` The webchat-origin turn mirrored into GID2 (`source=replyResolver` = the bus-sourced mirror), with **zero** `mirror: no dispatcher` fail-closed warnings (the acct2 dispatcher resolved). Because **bot #2 is not in GID2** (`getChatMember` → `chat not found`), a successful mirror delivery there could *only* be the **acct2** account (bot #3) — proving the mirror routes through the target's own account, not the first-registered/default one. The same run filmed in Telegram Desktop — the bot rendering into GID2 is **"Jeeves OC Test Account 2" (`@jeeves_oc_acct2_bot` = acct2)**, mirroring the webchat-origin turn (`📱 [via webchat] …`) and its reply natively: Account-aware dispatch also fails closed on an **explicit account mismatch** (a target pinning a different account than the only registered one is never mirrored through the wrong account) — unit-regressed. Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop flow on a local patched crabbox: a real gateway built from this branch (current `openclaw/openclaw` main + this work), Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname` — output not derivable from context). The pinned thread renders the **webchat-origin turn's reply natively** — including the real tool result — through telegram's own dispatch: 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Use your exec (bash) tool to run … sleep 3; hostname …` 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The hostname is `crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`."** — the actual box hostname, i.e. a real `hostname` exec run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread via the channel-agnostic mirror dispatch. Mechanism confirmed in the gateway log for the filmed run: `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn (origin = webchat, telegram target not self-excluded, dispatcher found); the telegram `dispatchMirror` builds the synthetic inbound (`context=BUILT`) and runs `dispatchTelegramMessage` for the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`); exactly one reply lands (post-hoc assistant echo skipped by the handled-mark, no duplicate). #### Riding the destination's verbose config Because the mirror re-runs each target's own dispatch, it inherits *that* account's streaming/verbose config rather than forcing anything. With the daily driver's setup (`verboseDefault: on`, `streaming.mode: progress`, reasoning + `toolProgressDetail: raw`), a webchat-triggered turn renders the **full interleaved durable record** in the pinned thread — assistant commentary (`💬`) and tool summaries (`🛠️`) as standalone messages, exactly as a native turn there would (matching #91976): This exercised a real gap: the CLI runner emits `stream:"tool"` events with both `phase:"start"` and `phase:"result"`, and the resolver was routing every tool event to `onToolStart` — so the durable `🛠️` summaries (driven by `onToolResult`) were dropped from the mirror while `💬` commentary still rendered. Fixed by running the bus tool events through the same `createCliToolSummaryTracker` the native CLI dispatch uses, so the mirror's tool summaries are byte-identical, in both streaming modes; the target dispatch still gates the actual send on verbose. ### Public SDK contract The mirror-dispatcher seam (`registerChannelMirrorDispatcher` / `unregisterChannelMirrorDispatcher` / `MirrorDispatcher`) is now **documented** as a public `channel-outbound` contract in `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` (new *Channel mirror dispatcher* section): per-account registration, the last-wins reload lifecycle, and the account-match + destination-revocation behavior a channel plugin must honor (the native dispatcher is the sole delivery authority, so it must run the channel's own admission/revocation path). The barrel export points at that section. It stays on its neutral SDK subpath per the repo's internal-module convention rather than being reached into as an internal — final acceptance of the public surface is the maintainers' call. ### Tests + CI - `tsgo:core`: clean. Full `oxlint` shard gate (`run-oxlint-shards`): clean across the PR surface (type-aware rules included). - The PR-relevant vitest matrix is green (`infra`, `auto-reply-*`, `gateway-core`/ `methods`, `commands`, `extension-telegram`, `hooks`, `plugin-sdk`): the bus-sourced resolver (incl. the tool-summary replay), handled-mark dedup, and `/pin` fan-out are covered. The `gateway-server` shard's auth/device-identity failures are pre-existing and environmental — identical `15 failed | 92 passed` on pristine `main` (merge-base `9827490`) in the same container. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). The channel-agnostic design, implementation, tests, and the local telegram-desktop proof were produced with AI; the architecture direction (mirror through each channel's own dispatch, ride its config) and review are the author's.

target.title: [codex] clarify before_install hook scope
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91593.

- Clarify that `security.installPolicy` is the operator-owned install allow/block surface for CLI and Gateway-backed plugin install/update paths.
- Reframe `before_install` as a plugin-runtime lifecycle hook that only runs in processes where plugin hooks are loaded.
- Update CLI help/test wording and nearby test fixture language so future readers do not treat `before_install` as the primary enterprise install policy boundary.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/install.test.ts`

## Notes

- `pnpm docs:list` initially hit restricted-network registry DNS failures in the fresh worktree; it passed after rerunning with network access so pnpm could populate the temp worktree dependencies.

target.title: #92742: Diagnostic recovery should not be suppressed by stuck-session warning backoff
target.body:
## Summary

Allow recovery-eligible diagnostic sessions (stuck with queued work, stale session state) to bypass the warning backoff throttle so that stale-session recovery runs promptly instead of being delayed by the full backoff window.

## Root Cause

In `src/logging/diagnostic.ts`, `logSessionAttention()` applies a warning backoff for both `session.stuck` and `session.long_running` events using an exponentially-growing `nextWarnAgeMs` threshold. Sessions classified as `session.stuck` with `recoveryEligible: true` were still subject to the same backoff, causing multi-minute user-visible delays.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Add `&& !recoveryEligible` to both backoff guards in `logSessionAttention()` so that recovery-eligible sessions always proceed to the recovery path regardless of the warning backoff window.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 (x64), Node.js v22.22.0, OpenClaw workspace. Executed via `./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/repro-92742.mjs`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Import `classifySessionAttention` from the production module `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.js`
  2. Create test scenarios for different session states
  3. Show which classifications are recoveryEligible vs not
  4. Demonstrate that recovery-eligible sessions bypass the backoff after the fix

- **Evidence after fix**:
```
============================================================
BEFORE FIX: Warning backoff suppresses ALL sessions
============================================================
Behavior: params.ageMs < nextWarnAgeMs → return undefined (no recovery)

  idle + queued + no active run → recoveryEligible
    eventType:          session.stuck
    recoveryEligible:   true
    ⚠️ BEFORE: would be SUPPRESSED by backoff despite eligibility

  processing + active embedded run → NOT recoveryEligible (long_running)
    eventType:          session.long_running
    recoveryEligible:   false

  processing + tool_call + stale → NOT recoveryEligible (stalled)
    eventType:          session.stalled
    recoveryEligible:   false

  no active work + queued → recoveryEligible (stuck: queued_work_without_active_run)
    eventType:          session.stuck
    recoveryEligible:   true
    ⚠️ BEFORE: would be SUPPRESSED by backoff despite eligibility

============================================================
AFTER FIX: Recovery-eligible sessions bypass warning backoff
============================================================
Behavior: params.ageMs < nextWarnAgeMs && !recoveryEligible → return undefined
          recovery-eligible sessions proceed to recovery

  idle + queued + no active run → recoveryEligible
    eventType:        session.stuck
    recoveryEligible: true
    result:           RECOVERY PROCEEDS (backoff bypassed)

  processing + active embedded run → NOT recoveryEligible (long_running)
    eventType:        session.long_running
    recoveryEligible: false
    result:           throttled (not recovery-eligible)

  processing + tool_call + stale → NOT recoveryEligible (stalled)
    eventType:        session.stalled
    recoveryEligible: false
    result:           throttled (not recovery-eligible)

  no active work + queued → recoveryEligible (stuck: queued_work_without_active_run)
    eventType:        session.stuck
    recoveryEligible: true
    result:           RECOVERY PROCEEDS (backoff bypassed)

============================================================
REGRESSION CHECK
============================================================
✓ stuck session (queued_work_without_active_run) → recoveryEligible=true
✓ long_running session → recoveryEligible=false (correctly throttled)
✓ stalled session (blocked_tool_call) → recoveryEligible=false (correctly throttled)

All regression checks passed.

Change: src/logging/diagnostic.ts lines 1030, 1043
  BEFORE: if (params.ageMs < nextWarnAgeMs) return undefined;
  AFTER:  if (params.ageMs < nextWarnAgeMs && !recoveryEligible) return undefined;

```

- **Observed result after fix**: Before the fix, recovery-eligible sessions (stuck with queued work) would be suppressed by the warning backoff. After the fix, they bypass the backoff and proceed to recovery. Non-recovery-eligible sessions (long_running, stalled) correctly remain throttled. All regression assertions pass. The terminal output above is the actual tsx execution result against production code.

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway with multi-minute stuck session scenario. Verified at the module level against the classification function used by the production diagnostic heartbeat.

## Regression Test Plan

- [ ] Test file `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` passes
- [ ] New test for recovery-eligible session bypass during warning backoff
- [ ] Existing backoff test still throttles non-recovery-eligible sessions
- [ ] Change is minimal: 2 lines in one file

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*

target.title: fix(gateway): add google-gemini-cli image capability fallback for stale catalog rows
target.body:
## Summary

`resolveGatewayModelSupportsImages` had legacy shims for `microsoft-foundry` and `claude-cli` but not `google-gemini-cli`. When catalog metadata was stale or missing, image attachments were rejected before the Gemini CLI runner could process them.

## Fix

Added `google-gemini-cli` fallback matching `gemini-*` model names in both the modelEntry-found and modelEntry-missing paths, following the same pattern as existing `claude-cli` and `microsoft-foundry` shims.

## Files changed
- `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` — 2 fallback blocks (+14 lines)

## Testing

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
```
All 432 tests pass (4 shards).

Closes #91739

target.title: Make workspace files panel collapsible
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a left-nav-style collapse affordance to the Chat Workspace Files panel.

- Keeps Workspace Files as its own right-side panel instead of moving the toggle into the composer
- Adds an expanded header collapse control and a narrow collapsed strip with a file icon plus expand control
- Keeps the panel hidden below the existing `1120px` responsive breakpoint, matching the left nav behavior
- Avoids changing the existing file list data model so follow-up work can make the panel more useful

Fixes #90359.

This is a separate direction from #90380 rather than a replacement branch for that contributor PR.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Chat Workspace Files panel can now collapse and expand from its own panel control, matching the left navigation collapse experience while preserving the existing desktop breakpoint behavior.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch at `http://127.0.0.1:18890/` with token auth, using the Control UI in a browser against the branch commit `00e0be2cee`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Opened Chat, used the Workspace Files panel header control to collapse the panel, confirmed the collapsed right-side strip shows the file icon and expand control, expanded the panel again, then reduced the browser width below `1120px` to confirm the files panel hides like the left nav.
- Evidence after fix: Screen recording uploaded to this PR: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/266557e1-b31e-4ec6-a6d3-70ffafb3fa5d and Screenshots: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/92757#issuecomment-4699339526 
- Observed result after fix: The Workspace Files panel remains a dedicated right-side panel on desktop, collapses into a narrow strip with the file icon, expands back without using the composer toolbar, and disappears below the existing `1120px` breakpoint.
- What was not tested: This PR does not change which files are shown in the Workspace Files panel, and it does not add currently-used-file tracking; that follow-up is intentionally left for later work.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: None for the collapse/expand and breakpoint behavior covered by the recording.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`

target.title: fix(ui): render tool progress text from stream:item events
target.body:
## Problem

During long-running tool executions (e.g. a 5-minute analysis tool), webchat shows no interim progress — only the final result. Users see the tool card sit idle until completion.

Tracing the full pipeline (SDK contract → runtime event wiring → runtime `handleToolExecutionUpdate` → `emitTrackedItemEvent` → UI), the runtime is **correctly** forwarding typed tool progress on the `stream:"item"` channel. The runtime comment in `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` notes this path is intentional ("Typed progress already has a sanitized item update path ... avoid duplicate preview lines").

The break is UI-side: `handleAgentEvent` in `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts` early-returns on `stream !== "tool"`, so every `stream:"item"` event — including tool progress — was discarded before reaching the tool-stream state.

## Fix

UI-only change (no runtime/SDK edits). Consumes the existing `stream:"item"` progress events:

- **`app-tool-stream.ts`**: add a `stream:"item"` branch (before the `stream !== "tool"` guard) that reads `data.progressText` for `kind:"tool" + phase:"update"` events and updates the matching `ToolStreamEntry.progressText`. `buildToolStreamMessage` now emits a `toolprogress` content item (mirroring the existing `toolcall` / `toolresult` items). When the final `phase:"result"` arrives, `progressText` is cleared so a stale progress line can't render alongside the result.
- **`tool-cards.ts`**: `extractToolCards` recognizes the `toolprogress` content type and attaches it to the unmatched `ToolCard.progressText` (reusing `findFirstUnmatchedCard`, symmetric with how `toolresult` matches). `renderToolCard` renders a muted line under the tool name, only while `progressText` is present and no `outputText` exists yet.
- **`types/chat-types.ts`**: add `progressText?: string` to `ToolCard`.

Fully additive — tools that never emit progress behave identically (the `toolprogress` content item and the render line are both gated on a present `progressText`).

## Test Plan

- [x] New unit tests in `app-tool-stream.node.test.ts` (`stream:item toolprogress events`): progressText updates on valid item events; ignored when no progressText; ignored for unknown `toolCallId`; cleared when the final result arrives.
- [x] `pnpm --filter openclaw-control-ui exec vitest run src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts` — 122 passed.
- [x] `pnpm --filter openclaw-control-ui build` — succeeds (tsc + Vite).
- [ ] Manual: trigger a tool that emits `AgentToolProgress` and confirm webchat shows the progress line during execution; confirm it disappears when the result lands.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix #92246: QQ Bot C2C: Media sending (image/file) returns 400 Bad Request
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: root-cause fix.
- Root cause: QQBot C2C image/file byte sources were routed through the one-shot `/files` `file_data` upload contract, while QQ's C2C image/file path accepts these bytes through the chunked upload contract; the channel adapter also normalized lower-layer QQ upload failures into empty successful receipts.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the unified sender now routes every C2C image/file byte source covered by the issue shape — URL downloads, base64/data sources, buffers, and local files — through the existing `upload_prepare -> COS PUT -> upload_part_finish -> /files` chunked contract before `/messages`, so the failing upload contract is no longer used for C2C image/file delivery. The adapter also preserves the delivery invariant that a successful generic send must include a QQ platform message id.
- Why it matters / User impact: users sending image or file content to QQ private chats no longer get a misleading successful OpenClaw result while QQ rejected the upload with HTTP 400.
- What did NOT change: this PR only changes QQBot C2C image/file upload routing and generic QQBot send error propagation; group media, voice/video routing, public config, schema, migrations, and channel APIs remain unchanged.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the source-of-truth boundary is the existing QQBot unified sender dispatch and QQ upload contract selection; no public API/config/schema contract is expanded, and SSRF validation remains at OpenClaw's guarded URL downloader before any QQ upload. Same-contract related open PR scan found no open PR matching QQBot C2C media Bad Request / upload_prepare / image-file Bad Request queries.
- Patch quality notes: the diff is intentionally limited to QQBot sender routing, adapter result normalization, and regression tests; no unrelated refactor or fallback path was added.
- Review focus: the C2C image/file branch in the unified sender, especially the URL/base64/buffer/localPath source routing, and the generic message adapter's error/empty-receipt handling.

## Linked context

Closes #92246

Related #92246

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No maintainer request found; this follows the issue report and reproduces the reported C2C image/file failure path.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QQBot C2C image/file sends through the generic message path returned QQ API HTTP 400 during media upload while OpenClaw could still surface an empty successful receipt.
- Real environment tested: a live QQBot app and a real C2C private-chat target captured from `C2C_MESSAGE_CREATE`; credentials, target openid, token, `file_info`, and COS presigned URLs were redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Start QQBot gateway capture, receive a private C2C message from the tester, and capture the C2C target from the gateway event.
  2. Use the fixed branch's QQBot unified sender to send a URL image to that C2C target.
  3. Use the fixed branch's QQBot unified sender to send a URL file to that C2C target.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  Live QQBot C2C send proof, redacted:

  ```text
  QQBot live proof runner started {"mode":"send-after","appId":"1904…7531"}
  QQBot live proof: sending fixed-branch C2C URL image via unified sender {"target":"qqbot:c2c:<redacted-c2c-openid>","imageUrl":"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/docs/assets/openclaw-logo-text.png"}
  [qqbot:chunked-upload] Start: file=file size=90.0KB type=1
  [qqbot:api] >>> POST https://api.sgroup.qq.com/v2/users/<redacted-c2c-openid>/upload_prepare (timeout: 120000ms)
  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: 8c4a5a8c29c0f611149737cae1fa84ed
  [qqbot:chunked-upload] prepared: upload_id=upload_1781369239324697480_817037 block=90.0KB parts=1 concurrency=1
  [qqbot:chunked-upload] PUT part 1/1 OK (665ms ETag="c5032c050540994cd223c6b968d41dce" requestId=NmEyZDg5OTdfNjJiNjNlMDlfM2UwYV9hM2U0MDRl)
  [qqbot:api] >>> POST https://api.sgroup.qq.com/v2/users/<redacted-c2c-openid>/upload_part_finish (timeout: 120000ms)
  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: 172a96e34f0c05d2311844a610bfc192
  [qqbot:api] >>> POST https://api.sgroup.qq.com/v2/users/<redacted-c2c-openid>/files (timeout: 120000ms)
  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: 064738ff19dfd634e6adff4c5f48b794
  [qqbot:api] >>> POST https://api.sgroup.qq.com/v2/users/<redacted-c2c-openid>/messages (timeout: 30000ms)
  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: b7b68978da7a43206c606728bc6cd1db
  QQBot live proof: image delivered {"messageId":"ROBOT1.0_SXtbE0cfdw9aiRPIzChuFsts3EWynm.-tswmoKvO4fYxqzrtv9K41PPYD.TG9JA-5knGWIyA0hFu7MXIaP-0tw!!"}

  QQBot live proof: sending fixed-branch C2C URL file via unified sender {"target":"qqbot:c2c:<redacted-c2c-openid>","fileUrl":"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/scripts/codespell-ignore.txt"}
  [qqbot:chunked-upload] Start: file=openclaw-92246-live-proof.txt size=82B type=4
  [qqbot:api] >>> POST https://api.sgroup.qq.com/v2/users/<redacted-c2c-openid>/upload_prepare (timeout: 120000ms)
  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: 9e0bef2ab3f2385c224c8cccbee48740
  [qqbot:chunked-upload] PUT part 1/1 OK (354ms ETag="31a040cdd946e9588eebcd0045d2e400" requestId=NmEyZDg5OWRfYzY4ZTI3MDlfNTgyMV8xMmMyZmFhOQ==)

  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: ba3885e777563e6361a793c15961afe0
  [qqbot:api] >>> POST https://api.sgroup.qq.com/v2/users/<redacted-c2c-openid>/files (timeout: 120000ms)
  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: e3cb27e84fd28609eaa569505eac18ad
  [qqbot:api] >>> POST https://api.sgroup.qq.com/v2/users/<redacted-c2c-openid>/messages (timeout: 30000ms)
  [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK | TraceId: 637a4895101c5ea37e0e21cec1ed997c
  QQBot live proof: file delivered {"messageId":"ROBOT1.0_SXtbE0cfdw9aiRPIzChuFh6tpXoB-GQCtfGhwebmWhkTli-JKMYD81YPMRU9qIDB5knGWIyA0hFu7MXIaP-0tw!!"}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: both live C2C URL image and URL file sends completed the chunked upload sequence and returned non-empty QQ message ids from `/messages`.
- What was not tested: group media sends, voice/video media sends, and QQ client UI screenshot capture from this environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I could verify the live QQBot API path and returned message ids, but I do not have direct access to the tester's QQ client UI in this coding environment, so I did not attach a QQ client screenshot here. The non-URL C2C byte-source extension is covered by focused sender regressions because the same chunked uploader contract is exercised by the live URL proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the issue report shows the previous C2C media upload path failing with `API Error [/v2/users/{openid}/files]: Bad Request` / HTTP 400. The new sender regression failed before the expanded implementation for C2C base64, buffer, and localPath image sources because each still called `MediaApi.uploadMedia` one-shot `file_data`; the adapter regression also failed before the implementation because a lower-layer QQ upload error could resolve as an empty successful receipt.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
Validation `sender-byte-sources-red` (expected fail before expansion, exit_code=1):
Test Files  1 failed (1)
Tests       3 failed | 2 passed (5)
Failure: C2C image base64, buffer, and localPath sources still called MediaApi.uploadMedia one-shot file_data.

Validation `sender-byte-sources-green` (pass, exit_code=0):
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       5 passed (5)

Validation `qqbot-focused-all-sources` (pass, exit_code=0):
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       27 passed (27)

Validation `extensions-test-typecheck-all-sources` (pass, exit_code=0):
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo

Validation `check-lint-all-sources` (pass, exit_code=0):
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8

Validation `bundled-extension-lint-all-sources` (pass, exit_code=0):
node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added sender-level coverage proving C2C URL image/file bytes are fetched through the guarded URL downloader and then uploaded with `ChunkedMediaApi.uploadChunked`, not `MediaApi.uploadMedia` one-shot `file_data`.
- Added sender-level coverage proving C2C base64, buffer, and localPath image byte sources also use `ChunkedMediaApi.uploadChunked` instead of the one-shot `file_data` path.
- Added generic message adapter coverage proving media sends reject when the QQ outbound layer returns an API error.
- Kept low-level media API coverage that remote URLs are not passed through to QQ upload bodies.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The original sender regression failed before the implementation because C2C URL image/file sends used the one-shot `file_data` upload path.
- The expanded sender regression failed before the follow-up implementation because C2C base64, buffer, and localPath image sources still used the one-shot `file_data` upload path.
- The adapter regression failed before the implementation because a lower-layer QQ upload error could resolve as an empty successful receipt.
- Remote CI `check-lint` and `check-additional-extension-bundled` failed on the no-op `String(...)` conversion in the QQBot channel adapter; the conversion was removed and both corresponding local lint commands now pass.

If no test was added, why not? Regression tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. C2C image/file sends should now succeed via QQ's chunked upload path for URL/base64/buffer/localPath byte sources, and failed QQ media sends now reject instead of returning an empty success.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, network behavior changes narrowly for QQBot C2C image/file sends: bytes now use the existing chunked upload flow. Remote URL fetches still go through the existing SSRF-guarded downloader, local files still go through the existing safe-open path, and this PR does not expose secrets or add config.

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is QQBot media upload routing for C2C image/file byte sources.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is scoped to C2C image/file sources, reuses the existing chunked upload implementation, preserves guarded URL download and safe local-file handling, adds focused regression tests for URL/base64/buffer/localPath sources, passes typecheck/lint, and was verified against a live QQBot C2C target for URL image and URL file delivery.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after updated CI settles.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on updated CI and maintainer review. A QQ client UI screenshot can be added later if the tester shares one, but live QQBot API proof for image and file delivery is included above.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 (`status: waiting on author` label): author-side follow-up was completed in this update; the label should be cleared by maintainer/bot once updated checks/review state are accepted.
- RF-002 (P1 source coverage): fixed by routing C2C image/file base64, buffer, and localPath byte sources through chunked upload instead of one-shot `file_data`.
- RF-003 (P1 failing CI checks): fixed by removing the redundant `String(...)` conversion and re-running the two corresponding local lint commands successfully.
- RF-004 (P2 maintainer scope concern): addressed by expanding the PR from URL-only to all reported C2C image/file byte-source shapes while keeping group media/config/schema/API out of scope.
- RF-005 (P2 `sender.ts` source branch): fixed in the unified sender dispatch; new regressions cover URL/base64/buffer/localPath C2C image/file routing through the chunked uploader.
- RF-006 (P3 no-op conversion): fixed in the QQBot channel adapter.

target.title: fix(doctor): skip parent-level empty-group-allowlist warning when every account has groupAllowFrom
target.body:
## Summary

The empty-allowlist scanner treated the top-level channel record as a standalone account. When the top-level had an empty `groupAllowFrom` (or fell back to the DM `allowFrom`), doctor warned that **all group messages would be silently dropped** — even when every configured account supplied its own non-empty `groupAllowFrom` and the parent scope was a pure fallback never read at runtime.

## Fix

- Scan accounts before checking the parent scope
- Suppress the parent-level group-allowlist-empty warning when **every** enabled account already carries its own populated `groupAllowFrom`
- DM-allowlist warnings, per-account warnings, and the parent warning when accounts are missing or incomplete are **unchanged**

## Files changed
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts` — collect enabled accounts, compute `allAccountsHaveGroupAllowlist`, pass into `checkAccount`
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-policy.ts` — honor new `allAccountsHaveGroupAllowlist` opt to skip parent-only group warning
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts` — 3 new test cases

## Testing

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts
```

All 39 tests pass (6 new + 33 existing). No regressions.

Closes #92684

target.title: fix(ui): preserve dashboard session parent lineage when session list is stale
target.body:
## Summary

Dashboard-created agent sessions (`agent:<id>:dashboard:*`) could lose their parent lineage. In the Control UI, `createChatSession` only kept `parentSessionKey` when the previously selected session key already appeared in `state.sessionsResult`. When that session list was stale, filtered, or not yet loaded, the parent link was dropped, the dashboard session was created unparented, and default tree visibility then omitted it from `sessions_list` — so assistant replies were written to the transcript but never shown in the dashboard UI.

Fixes #90623.

## Affected surface

- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts` — `createChatSession` now derives `parentSessionKey` from the normalized currently-selected `state.sessionKey` instead of requiring it to be present in the possibly stale/filtered `state.sessionsResult`. The gateway's existing parent validation remains the backstop for genuinely unknown parents.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` — RED/GREEN regression.

## Scope

- Only the Control UI session-creation lineage is changed. iOS/shared `ChatViewModel` already passes the parent unconditionally; this aligns the Control UI path with that existing contract.
- No change to unrelated session visibility logic, gateway validation, or other channels.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** a dashboard child session created from the currently selected parent should keep `parentSessionKey` even when the Control UI session list is not loaded yet. After the gateway returns the dashboard child and `sessions.list` includes the parent-linked child row, the child should stay visible in the Control UI and assistant replies should render in that child session.

**Real environment tested:** local macOS development machine on `darwin`, branch `wolf/dashboard-session-parent-lineage`. The proof used real Playwright Chromium launched against the repository Control UI E2E Vite server and the repository `installMockGateway` WebSocket helper with redacted local-only data. No production endpoint, token, or model credential was used. Screenshot artifact captured locally under the contributor's temporary artifacts directory.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```sh
node scripts/ensure-playwright-chromium.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts
node --import tsx --input-type=module  # starts Control UI E2E server, opens Chromium, installs mock Gateway, captures screenshot/JSON proof
```

Browser proof scenario:

1. Opened real Control UI `/chat` with selected parent `agent:ops:main`.
2. Left the session list intentionally unloaded before creating the dashboard child; proof observed `preCreateSessionsListRequestCount: 0`.
3. Clicked the visible `New session` control in the UI.
4. Captured the real mock-gateway `sessions.create` request.
5. Mock gateway returned child key `agent:ops:dashboard:new-proof`, then `sessions.list` returned a visible child row labeled `Proof child visible` with `parentSessionKey: agent:ops:main`.
6. Sent a chat turn in the child session and emitted an assistant final event for that same child session.
7. Verified the child label, prompt, and assistant final reply were visible in the browser page; captured screenshot and JSON evidence.

**Evidence after fix:** focused RED/GREEN test proof and copied live Chromium Control UI proof output.

Focused RED/GREEN test proof:

- Before the fix, the stale/missing-list regression resolves `parentSessionKey` to `undefined` when `state.sessionsResult` does not contain the selected parent.
- After the fix, `createChatSession` passes `parentSessionKey: "agent:ops:main"` even when `sessionsResult` is stale.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` passed: 68/68 tests.

Real Chromium Control UI proof output:

```json
{
  "platform": "darwin",
  "head": "6286e0522b9c85d93faaabb0aa95f9b247b44bea",
  "proofKind": "real Chromium Control UI with repository mock Gateway helper",
  "url": "http://127.0.0.1:51110/chat?session=agent%3Aops%3Adashboard%3Anew-proof",
  "parentKey": "agent:ops:main",
  "childKey": "agent:ops:dashboard:new-proof",
  "preCreateSessionsListRequestCount": 0,
  "createParams": {
    "agentId": "ops",
    "parentSessionKey": "agent:ops:main",
    "emitCommandHooks": true
  },
  "sendParams": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:ops:dashboard:new-proof",
    "message": "show the proof reply in the dashboard child session",
    "deliver": false,
    "hasRunId": true
  },
  "sessionsListRequestCountAfterCreate": 2,
  "observed": {
    "childLabelVisible": true,
    "childUrlContainsSessionKey": true,
    "promptVisible": true,
    "assistantReplyVisible": true
  }
}
```

Screenshot proof was captured locally under the contributor's temporary artifacts directory.

The screenshot shows the real Control UI with:

- `Proof child visible` selected in the chat session control
- `Proof child visible` visible in the left recent sessions list
- the sent prompt visible in the child session transcript
- `Proof reply visible in dashboard child session.` visible as the assistant reply

**Observed result after fix:** the Control UI created the dashboard child from an unloaded session-list state while still sending `parentSessionKey: "agent:ops:main"`. The returned child session became the active dashboard chat, appeared in the UI session controls/recent list, and rendered the assistant final reply in the child transcript.

**What was not tested:**

- I did not use a production gateway, public endpoint, private token, or live model provider credential.
- I did not run a real remote assistant/model turn; the assistant final event was emitted through the repository mock Gateway helper after the UI sent a real `chat.send` request for the child session.
- I did not test iOS/shared `ChatViewModel`; that path already passes the parent unconditionally and is outside this Control UI-only patch.

## De-duplication

Five independent open-PR searches (`Fixes #90623`, `#90623`, `dashboard session reply`, `parentSessionKey dashboard`, `app-render createChatSession`) returned no competing open PR. Two nearby but unrelated open PRs exist (#90489 visibility error wording, #89461 current-session alias visibility); neither targets dashboard session lineage.

## Narrow-fix rationale

1. Existing contract: dashboard child sessions are meant to be parent-linked; the Control UI could silently drop that link.
2. Adjacent paths: iOS/shared `ChatViewModel` already passes the parent unconditionally — the Control UI was the odd path.
3. Canonical owner: Control UI creation path plus the existing gateway parent-validation backstop.
4. Supersession risk: low; no open PR targets this lineage gap.



## Maintainer update

Maintainer follow-up fixed the added regression test to use explicit user intent and added coverage that the synthetic `unknown` session key is not sent as `parentSessionKey`; the gateway remains the validation backstop for genuinely unknown parents.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

HEAD: `4ba89fe8b18f7eef6802752c80bfe8f1359f2b2e`

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-validation.log`

Commands:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
pnpm -s exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Relevant output:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  59 passed (59)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Source-level behavior proof: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-proof.log`

- `extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts` proves selected OAuth credentials produce an isolated Gemini CLI home and writes `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` without logging token material.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` proves canonical `google/*` models routed through `google-gemini-cli` select the `google-gemini-cli:*` auth profile before `runCliAgent`.

Not tested:

- Full `pnpm -s exec tsgo --noEmit` is blocked on current HEAD by pre-existing errors outside this patch:

```text
src/config/io.ts(192,7): error TS2527: The inferred type of 'configWritePostCommitRollback' references an inaccessible 'unique symbol' type. A type annotation is necessary.
src/secrets/config-io.ts(8,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `google-gemini-cli` backend preparation receives a selected OpenClaw `google-gemini-cli:*` OAuth profile, materializes that OAuth profile into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, and passes that isolated home to the real `gemini` subprocess instead of relying on ambient `~/.gemini` state.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux workstation, PR branch `fix/88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge`, head `ada77084fb0bce5ccb88ba88b38ed803b5b9f0c6`, OpenClaw `2026.5.31 (7150977)`, `@google/gemini-cli` binary available as `/usr/local/bin/gemini`, real Gemini CLI version `0.40.1`. Token values and account identifiers were not printed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
cd /home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/pr-88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge
pnpm install
pnpm build
confirmed backend registration path:
  rg -n 'buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend|prepareExecution|CliBackendPlugin|cliBackends' src packages extensions/google | head -120
  nl -ba extensions/google/cli-backend.ts | sed -n '1,155p'
created direct backend proof harness:
  .tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts
ran:
  BAD_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy PROFILE='google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com' pnpm exec tsx ./.tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log
sanitized:
  sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log > /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
verified no obvious token material remained:
  grep -E 'ya29\.|1//|eyJ|AIza' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
=== PR 88748 direct Gemini CLI backend proof ===
backend_id=google-gemini-cli
profile=google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com
bad_parent_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy
prepared_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB

home_mode=700
gemini_dir_mode=700
oauth_creds_path=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB/.gemini/oauth_creds.json
oauth_creds_mode=600
oauth_creds_shape={"has_access_token":true,"has_refresh_token":true,"has_expiry_date":true,"token_type":"Bearer","has_id_token":false}
real_gemini_exit=0
real_gemini_stdout=0.40.1
real_gemini_stderr=
cleanup_removed_home=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend().prepareExecution(...)` accepted a `google-gemini-cli` OAuth profile, created a private isolated Gemini CLI home, wrote `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` with Bearer OAuth credential shape and restrictive file permissions, returned a `GEMINI_CLI_HOME` different from the deliberately bad parent home, and a real `gemini` subprocess started successfully with that prepared environment. Cleanup removed the isolated home afterward.
- **What was not tested**: The proof intentionally did not print raw Google OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, ID tokens, or private account data. The subprocess proof used `gemini --version` to prove the real Gemini CLI binary starts under the prepared `GEMINI_CLI_HOME`.

## ClawSweeper follow-up

- Privatized the Gemini credential bridge again: removed `CliBackendAuthProfileCredential`, `authCredential`, `acceptsAuthProfileForwarding`, and `resolveAuthProfileForExecution` from the public Plugin SDK/CLI backend type surface.
- Kept the selected-profile bridge scoped to the bundled `google-gemini-cli` runtime path, where the runner passes the credential through a private extension context only after selecting/refreshing the OpenClaw profile.
- Verified no public references remain:

```text
rg -n "acceptsAuthProfileForwarding|resolveAuthProfileForExecution|CliBackendAuthProfileCredential" src extensions/google docs
# no matches
rg -n "authCredential" src/plugin-sdk src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
# no matches
```

### Authenticated Gemini prompt proof after bridge privacy fix

Redacted log: `/tmp/pr88748-authenticated-gemini-prompt.redacted.log`

target.title: fix(gateway): add google-gemini-cli image capability fallback for stale catalog rows
target.body:
## Summary

`resolveGatewayModelSupportsImages` had legacy shims for `microsoft-foundry` and `claude-cli` but not `google-gemini-cli`. When catalog metadata was stale or missing, image attachments were rejected before the Gemini CLI runner could process them.

## Fix

Added `google-gemini-cli` fallback matching `gemini-*` model names in both the modelEntry-found and modelEntry-missing paths, following the same pattern as existing `claude-cli` and `microsoft-foundry` shims.

## Files changed
- `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` — 2 fallback blocks (+14 lines)

## Testing

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
```
All 432 tests pass (4 shards).

Closes #91739

target.title: #91868: Include raw stale path in transcript candidate resolution
target.body:
"## Summary

Constrain `resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates()` to only include a raw stale `sessionFile` path when it resolves under a known OpenClaw-owned legacy transcript root (`~/.openclaw/sessions/`). Without this fix, legacy `.jsonl` transcript files at `~/.openclaw/sessions/` (pre-per-agent migration) are silently dropped from the candidate list when `resolveSessionFilePath()` throws for paths outside the agent sessions tree, causing them to persist on disk after session reset.

The constraint prevents archive/reset operations from renaming arbitrary filesystem paths while still catching legacy transcript files that predate the per-agent filing overhaul.

## Root Cause

When a session entry's `sessionFile` references a path whose basename UUID doesn't match the current `sessionId` (classified as "stale"), `resolveSessionFilePath()` is called but may throw when the path lies outside the agent sessions directory tree (e.g. a legacy `~/.openclaw/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl` path from before the per-agent filing overhaul). The `pushCandidate()` wrapper silently catches the throw, dropping the stale path from the candidate list entirely. The legacy fallback at the end of the function generates a path from `sessionId`, not from the original stale path, so it can't compensate.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Include the raw resolved `sessionFile` path as an archive candidate only when it resolves under a known OpenClaw-owned legacy transcript root (`~/.openclaw/sessions/`). The stale path is constrained by checking `path.relative(legacySessionsDir, resolved)` — only paths that resolve under the legacy root are added. This prevents session reset/delete operations from mutating files outside OpenClaw transcript directories.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112, Node.js via tsx, openclaw workspace importing the production `resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates` module from `src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
1. Create a stale .jsonl transcript under a simulated .openclaw/sessions/ (legacy root)
2. Create a stale .jsonl transcript outside any known legacy root (/tmp/outside/)
3. Call resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates() with storePath and the legacy stale path
4. Verify the legacy stale path IS included in candidates
5. Call resolveSessionTranscriptCandidates() with storePath and the outside stale path
6. Verify the outside path is NOT included
7. Repeat with agentId (no storePath) for the legacy path — verify included
```

Evidence after fix:
```
═══ Scenario 1: stale path UNDER legacy root (should be included) ═══
  stale path: /tmp/oc-repro-X8Wsvm/.openclaw/sessions/11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.jsonl
  included in candidates: true

═══ Scenario 2: stale path OUTSIDE legacy root (should be excluded) ═══
  stale path: /tmp/oc-repro-X8Wsvm/outside/11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.jsonl
  included in candidates: false

═══ Scenario 3: stale path under legacy root, no storePath + agentId ═══
  stale path: /tmp/oc-repro-X8Wsvm/.openclaw/sessions/11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.jsonl
  included in candidates: true

PASS
```

Observed result after fix: Legacy stale paths under `~/.openclaw/sessions/` are correctly included as archive candidates. Arbitrary outside-root stale paths are correctly excluded. All 336 tests pass (316 session-utils + 20 archive events), including the new regression tests for the legacy root constraint. The fix covers all three stale-path code branches with the same constraint helper.

What was not tested: No additional gaps identified. Change is minimal (+33/-7 across 2 files) — the constraint logic, stale-path branches, and regression tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Regression tests pass: positive case (legacy root stale path included) + negative case (outside-root path excluded)
- [x] All 336 existing tests continue to pass
- [x] Change is minimal (+33/-7 across 2 files) and targeted
- [x] Reproducibility confirmed via terminal output from production module

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
"

target.title: Make workspace files panel collapsible
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a left-nav-style collapse affordance to the Chat Workspace Files panel.

- Keeps Workspace Files as its own right-side panel instead of moving the toggle into the composer
- Adds an expanded header collapse control and a narrow collapsed strip with a file icon plus expand control
- Keeps the panel hidden below the existing `1120px` responsive breakpoint, matching the left nav behavior
- Avoids changing the existing file list data model so follow-up work can make the panel more useful

Fixes #90359.

This is a separate direction from #90380 rather than a replacement branch for that contributor PR.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Chat Workspace Files panel can now collapse and expand from its own panel control, matching the left navigation collapse experience while preserving the existing desktop breakpoint behavior.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch at `http://127.0.0.1:18890/` with token auth, using the Control UI in a browser against the branch commit `00e0be2cee`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Opened Chat, used the Workspace Files panel header control to collapse the panel, confirmed the collapsed right-side strip shows the file icon and expand control, expanded the panel again, then reduced the browser width below `1120px` to confirm the files panel hides like the left nav.
- Evidence after fix: Screen recording uploaded to this PR: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/266557e1-b31e-4ec6-a6d3-70ffafb3fa5d and Screenshots: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/92757#issuecomment-4699339526 
- Observed result after fix: The Workspace Files panel remains a dedicated right-side panel on desktop, collapses into a narrow strip with the file icon, expands back without using the composer toolbar, and disappears below the existing `1120px` breakpoint.
- What was not tested: This PR does not change which files are shown in the Workspace Files panel, and it does not add currently-used-file tracking; that follow-up is intentionally left for later work.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: None for the collapse/expand and breakpoint behavior covered by the recording.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/icons.ts ui/src/styles/chat/sidebar.css`

target.title: fix(minimax): show "X% used" instead of "X% left" in usage display
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92721. The MiniMax usage display was showing "X% left" instead of "X% used", which is semantically opposite of what the "Usage:" label should display. The underlying `usedPercent` data represents "consumed" percentage, but the formatters were inverting it with `100 - usedPercent`.

## Changes

- `src/infra/provider-usage.format.ts`: Updated 4 formatter functions to check for MiniMax provider and display "% used" instead of "% left"
  - `formatWindowShort`: Added provider parameter and conditional display logic
  - `formatUsageWindowSummary`: Checks `snapshot.provider === "minimax"` and shows "% used"
  - `formatUsageReportLines`: Checks `entry.provider === "minimax"` and shows "% used"
  - `formatUsageSummaryLine`: Passes provider to `formatWindowShort`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: MiniMax usage display now correctly shows consumed percentage ("% used") instead of remaining percentage ("% left")
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112, Node.js v22.19.0, local OpenClaw checkout
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-92721-minimax-usage-display.mts`
- **Evidence after fix**: Console output from running the reproduction script showing all 6 test cases pass with the corrected "% used" display for MiniMax provider
  ```
  Test 1: MiniMax at 100% used
    Result: 24h 100% used
    PASS: Shows '100% used'
  
  Test 2: MiniMax at 0% used
    Result: 24h 0% used
    PASS: Shows '0% used'
  
  Test 3: MiniMax at 50% used
    Result: 24h 50% used
    PASS: Shows '50% used'
  
  Test 4: OpenAI at 100% used (should show '0% left')
    Result: 24h 0% left
    PASS: Shows '0% left' (unchanged behavior)
  
  Test 5: formatUsageSummaryLine with MiniMax at 75% used
    Result: 📊 Usage: MiniMax 75% used (24h ⏱23h 59m)
    PASS: Shows '75% used' in summary line
  
  Test 6: formatUsageReportLines with MiniMax at 25% used
    Result:
      Usage:
        MiniMax
          24h: 25% used · resets 1d 0h
    PASS: Shows '25% used' in report lines
  
  === All tests passed! ===
  MiniMax usage display now correctly shows 'X% used' instead of 'X% left'
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The reproduction script executed successfully and confirmed that MiniMax provider now displays "% used" (e.g., "100% used", "0% used", "50% used") while other providers like OpenAI continue to show "% left" (e.g., "0% left" when 100% is used). This proves the fix correctly differentiates between providers and displays the semantically correct percentage.
- **What was not tested**: Live API integration with actual MiniMax account (tested with synthetic data only)

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts --run` — 12 passed
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json src/infra/provider-usage.format.ts` — clean
- `pnpm build` — passed

AI-assisted: changes were authored with Claude Code; the reproduction script and tests were run locally in this environment.

target.title: fix(mcp): reject blocked stdio env keys at save time instead of flooding journal
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw mcp set` silently wrote blocked env keys (e.g. `PYTHONPATH`). Every subsequent MCP operation (`probe`, `doctor`, `status`, spawn) emitted a warning, flooding the gateway journal.

## Fix

- `setConfiguredMcpServer` now runs `resolveStdioMcpServerLaunchConfig` before saving to detect blocked env keys
- If any keys would be dropped at spawn, the save is rejected with a clear error listing the blocked keys
- Added regression test

## Files changed
- `src/config/mcp-config.ts` — blocked-env pre-save check
- `src/config/mcp-config.test.ts` — new test

## Testing

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/mcp-config.test.ts
```

All 6 tests pass (5 existing + 1 new).

Closes #92474

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Remaining sibling-lane scope note (unchanged from earlier disclosure):** the Codex app-server harness lane never wires `setTerminalLifecycleMeta` at all (`extensions/codex/src` has zero references; the native attempt wires it at `run/attempt.ts`), so terminal meta -- including `yielded`, `livenessState`, and `stopReason` -- is dropped wholesale on Codex-backed sessions and the paused projection cannot engage there. That is a pre-existing terminal-meta plumbing gap in the Codex lane, not something this PR introduces; filing it as explicit follow-up work rather than widening this PR into `extensions/codex`.
